message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "high-enchantment (>=8) item: 50% reimbursement then deductible"
Passing tests so far: 24

Test: sword enchantment 9, damage 1000. Expected payout 400 (1000 * 0.5 = 500, -100 deductible).

Implement payout per damage entry:
- Look up the actual item in the policy by itemType (use the first matching one in policy items).
- If item.enchantment >= 8: damage *= 0.5
- If item.material === "dragon": no reduction (full reimbursement); spec says dragon overrides... actually spec says BOTH clauses apply, but 50% rule wins per the example "dragon, ench 9, damage 1000 → payout 400". So check enchantment >= 8 FIRST (50% applies), then dragon is not relevant in that case. For dragon without high ench, full. For non-dragon + high-ench, 50%.
  Actually re-read: "Damage to items with enchantment level ≥ 8 is reimbursed at 50 % of the damage amount. Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed."
  Example: "dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G → payout 400 G (both clauses apply; the 50 % rule wins)".
  So if enchantment >= 8, use 50%. Else if dragon, use full. Else, use full damage.
  Actually for plain item, also full damage (just deductible). So logic:
    base_reimbursement = (enchantment >= 8) ? damage * 0.5 : damage
- Then subtract 100 deductible per damage entry.
- Floor (round down).

For now we have only one damage per claim. Keep that. Future tests will multiply.

Don't break existing tests. The previous claim test "regular sword 500 G damage" still expects payout 400. With our logic: enchantment 3, so no 50%. damage=500, -100=400. ✓
Rune 200 damage: rune has no enchantment so undefined, 200-100=100. ✓

Run `pnpm test`. Working dir: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current state and implement the minimal change needed.
Now I'll implement the minimal change for high-enchantment 50% reimbursement.

content.text:
All 25 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Updated `processClaim` to look up each damaged item in the policy by `itemType` and apply a 50% reduction to the damage amount when the item's enchantment is >= 8, before subtracting the deductible per damage entry.
- Result: All tests now pass (25 passing, 11 todo).
- Approach: Minimal change. Used `policy.items.find` to locate the item, then a ternary on `enchantment >= 8` to halve damage when applicable. Existing tests still pass because enchantment 3 (sword test) and undefined enchantment (rune test) fall through to full damage. Kept the per-entry `(reimbursement - DEDUCTIBLE)` summation since the test still uses one damage entry; this naturally accommodates future multi-entry tests without over-implementing further (no cap enforcement, no item-pool tracking, no dragon material).

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_22-34-24_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking-2/src/claim-office.spec.ts
